public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as supply marshal.
appearance: Quota-practical armor, work-hardened layers, and visibly maintained equipment mark function before ceremony.
manner: Balances command voice with practiced observation, sounding like someone who expects trouble to hide inside normality. In Soreheim Alliance, that reads as someone quota-aware, contribution-minded, and structurally pragmatic, filtering choices through communal ambition, and the habits of a supply marshal still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of supply marshal in and around Artificers’ Guild, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Artificers’ Guild workable through the obligations of supply marshal while managing the immediate pressure of resource depletion in Soreheim Alliance.
hidden_pressure: Must notice trouble early enough that force remains legitimate rather than merely reactive. Around Artificers’ Guild, that usually means carrying resource depletion, sabotage, predatory frontier ecology without letting the role of supply marshal visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Varik Ironslope can draw the party into contraband, false papers, or a suspicious cargo problem tied to Artificers’ Guild.
Any task accepted from Varik Ironslope may turn into an escort, checkpoint, or route-control problem before the destination is reached.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. Appearance extended under V33_1 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication. V33_1 additive depth pass preserved canon while strengthening at-table identity, appearance, and social motive.
public_signal: Uniform, posture, and the way others make room mark Varik Ironslope as visible enforcement before rank ever needs to be named.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Artificers’ Guild: checkpoints, barracks, patrol seams, or visible trouble-spots
encounter_context.interaction_anchor: threshold control or rapid incident response

first_impression: Varik Ironslope presents as Balances command voice with practiced observation, sounding like someone who expects trouble to hide inside normality. In Soreheim Alliance, that reads as someone quota-aware, contribution-minded, and structurally pragmatic, filtering choices through communal ambition, and the habits of a supply marshal still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Supply Marshal, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Soreheim Alliance.
what_they_refuse: Varik Ironslope refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Supply Marshal too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Varik Ironslope can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Varik Ironslope adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Varik Ironslope is Known locally through day-to-day work as supply marshal. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Supply Marshal.
